own_review.issue: gluonhq/setup-graalvm pinned to mutable @master branch (with GITHUB_TOKEN in env) — supply-chain and reproducibility risk; pin to a tag or commit SHA like the CLI's graalvm/setup-graalvm@v1

PR adds reachability-metadata.json (reflection+jni) but leaves legacy reflect-config.json (521 lines) and jni-config.json (123 lines) in the same auto-discovered directory — duplicate/possibly conflicting metadata; GraalVM for JDK 23 warns and may ignore legacy files, silently dropping entries not in the new file. Should consolidate.

Pinning to non-LTS JDK 23 (EOL Sep 2025) via download.oracle.com/graalvm/23/latest and setup-java distribution 'oracle': Oracle only serves /latest for the current feature release and LTS, so these will 404/fail after JDK 24 GA — a recurring-maintenance trap; an LTS (21) or GraalVM Community would be more durable
GUI builds lose Maven dependency caching: graalvm/setup-graalvm had cache:'maven', gluonhq/setup-graalvm has no cache support and no actions/cache step was added — slower CI, no correctness impact

File minified to a single line (unreviewable diffs); new 'globs' array is internally inconsistent with the retained 'includes' patterns (omits SplashLight.png, Backgrounds/DriftyMainLight.png, DrawRoundRect_Color.frag, Blend_SRC_IN.frag, LinearConvolveShadow_20/_4.frag) — harmless today because the includes section still lists them, but confusing generated output; bundles also dropped the "" (default-locale) entry

arch: 'amd64' is not a documented input value of gluonhq/setup-graalvm (docs say x86_64/aarch64); it happens to work because the action maps any non-aarch64 value to -amd64 for java23, and Docker images are amd64-only anyway, but it is inconsistent with build.yml's 'x86_64' and arguably unnecessary (matches the human reviewer's point)

Windows profile pins gluonfx-maven-plugin back to 1.0.23 (1.0.25 cannot build native images on Windows) — reasonable workaround, well-commented, but Windows/macOS builds are explicitly untested per the PR description, so the 1.0.23 + Gluon GraalVM java23 combination is an unverified risk

points.point: GUI/pom.xml:51 empty <version> tag for gluonfx-maven-plugin breaks GUI build
points.note: Fabricated: the full diff clearly shows <version>${gluonfx-maven-plugin.version}</version> with the property defined as 1.0.25
Fabricated: the diff updates exactly those lines to <source>23</source>/<target>23</target>

points.point: CLI/pom.xml:48,88,128,168 native-maven-plugin 0.10.4 does not support JDK 23; support added in 0.10.5 (May 2024)
points.note: Line refs are real (0.10.4 at those lines) but the incompatibility and dates are fabricated: 0.10.4 (Dec 2024) postdates JDK 23 GA (Sep 2024) and supports it; the author's Ubuntu CLI build succeeded with this exact setup
The update is plainly in the diff; hedged non-finding

points.point: resource-config.json ICU icudt75b speculation for Java 23
points.note: Speculative; JDK 23 uses icudt74b which is already present

missed: human: docker-publish.yml arch change unnecessary (amd64-only releases)
legacy reflect-config.json/jni-config.json left alongside new reachability-metadata.json
non-LTS JDK 23 EOL / Oracle 'latest' URL breakage (Dockerfile, oracle setup-java distro)
resource-config.json globs/locale inconsistencies (dropped "" locale, omitted entries)

points.point: Stale reflect-config.json/jni-config.json not removed alongside new reachability-metadata.json
points.note: Verified: both files exist at base in the auto-discovered META-INF/native-image dir and are untouched by the diff; exact line counts (521/123) are correct

points.point: globs array omits DrawRoundRect_Color.frag, LinearConvolveShadow_20.frag, Blend_SRC_IN.frag
points.note: Textually accurate about the globs array, but the retained 'includes' patterns section still lists all three, so no resources are actually lost; appropriately hedged ('verify or add back')
Verified in diff: old locales ["","en-US","und"] vs new ["en-US","und"]; plausible localization fallback regression on non-en-US systems

points.point: Windows 1.0.23 workaround comment lacks issue-tracker URL
gluonhq/setup-graalvm@master mutable pin, should be tag/SHA
points.note: Verified; real supply-chain/reproducibility issue, matches my own finding

points.point: CodeQL uses Oracle JDK rather than GraalVM

missed: human: docker-publish.yml arch change unnecessary (amd64-only releases)
non-LTS JDK 23 EOL / Oracle 'latest' URL breakage (Dockerfile, oracle setup-java distro)
exceeds: Stale legacy native-image config files (not in human reference; verified real)
Lost default-locale bundle entry (not in human reference; verified real)
@master mutable action pin (not in human reference; verified real)

qualitative: v4 is dominated by three confidently-asserted 'critical' findings that are all false against the full diff: the 'empty <version> tag' and 'compiler still hardcodes 21' absence-fabrications (both flagged in the brief and confirmed false here), plus a fabricated native-maven-plugin 0.10.4/JDK-23 incompatibility with invented release dates, while missing everything the human and my review caught. v5 repeats neither of v4's fabrications and instead produces three verified, diff-grounded findings (stale legacy config files with exact line counts, the dropped default-locale bundle entry, the @master mutable action pin), all of which exceed the thin one-point human reference. v5's weaknesses are mild: several process/documentation nits with no scoring value, an overcautious shader-resource warning whose entries are actually still covered by the retained includes section, and it misses the human's amd64-arch point and the non-LTS JDK 23 EOL / Oracle download-URL sustainability trap. Net, v5 is a clear and large improvement over v4 on this PR: accurate where v4 hallucinated, and genuinely additive beyond the reference review.
